description: Image-only 'fake scanned' PDFs rasterized from data/raw/ originals. No text layer -- generated by scripts/make_fake_scans.py. Ground truth is NOT duplicated here: score OCR output against data/ground_truth/<same-stem>.json, since these are pixel-identical page content to the originals (only rendering/compression/geometry differs).

documents.why_chosen: 29 pages, dense text, boxed warning -- long/dense stress test at otherwise-pristine quality
42 pages, dense oncology label with large tables -- second long/dense stress test at otherwise-pristine quality
simulates a slightly crooked feed through a low-end document scanner

documents.degradation: low resolution + skew + gaussian blur/noise + heavy JPEG compression
documents.why_chosen: worst-case realistic quality -- old fax-machine-quality scan; also the shortest doc (10 pages) so it's cheap to iterate on while tuning OCR settings

verification: Each output was checked for (1) num_pages matching the source PDF exactly, (2) zero extractable text via pymupdf_extractor.extract_pdf (full_text length == 0, confirming no residual text layer), and (3) visual inspection of a rendered sample page per document confirming the intended rotation/blur/resolution actually took effect.
